issue_title: Package using setup.cfg metadata fails within virtualenv due to outdated setuptools
issue_body:
I'm currently in the process of refactoring a lot of Python code into multiple proper Python packages. What I noticed quickly is that each modules "setup.py" was a slightly modified copy&paste. Fortunately I found that setuptools allows to factor everything variable into a separate setup.cfg:

  https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/setuptools.html#configuring-setup-using-setup-cfg-files

The feature needs a setuptools >= December 2016. Not a problem in early 2018 I thought ;-)

When I install the package inside a virtual environment (e.g. to run tox tests) all kinds of odd effects happen. This is a demo of the effect:

```
E:\temp>mkdir venv

E:\temp>c:\Python36\python.exe -m venv venv

E:\temp>venv\Scripts\activate
(venv) E:\temp>pip install e:\Dev\Pype\cstutil\dist\cstutil-0.0.zip
Processing e:\dev\pype\cstutil\dist\cstutil-0.0.zip
Installing collected packages: UNKNOWN
  Running setup.py install for UNKNOWN ... done
Successfully installed UNKNOWN-0.0

(venv) E:\temp>pip install --upgrade setuptools
Collecting setuptools
  Using cached setuptools-38.4.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Installing collected packages: setuptools
  Found existing installation: setuptools 28.8.0
    Uninstalling setuptools-28.8.0:
      Successfully uninstalled setuptools-28.8.0
Successfully installed setuptools-38.4.0

(venv) E:\temp>pip install e:\Dev\Pype\cstutil\dist\cstutil-0.0.zip
Processing e:\dev\pype\cstutil\dist\cstutil-0.0.zip
Collecting six (from cstutil==0.0)
  Using cached six-1.11.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Requirement already satisfied: setuptools>=30.3.0 in e:\temp\venv\lib\site-packages (from cstutil==0.0)
Installing collected packages: six, cstutil
  Running setup.py install for cstutil ... done
Successfully installed cstutil-0.0 six-1.11.0
```

As you can see the old setuptools version just ignores the metadata. What makes matters worse is that the requirement "setuptools >= 30.3.0" of the package also doesn't work! 

After several "pip install --upgrade setuptools" runs and a lot of scratching my head I discovered that the old setuptools come from within virtualenv.  Is there a chance that the version can be bumped?

Using "install_requires" as parameter in setup.py would allow me to express the setuptools-dependency in a way setuptools 28 understands. Then however setting the (package specific) list of other dependencies would need to go into setup.py again -- defying the whole idea of pushing this information into setup.cfg.

issue_title: `fluent_pages.0001_initial` migration makes wrong assumption about `SITE_ID`.
issue_body:
It assumes that if any `Site` objects exist, that a site matching `settings.SITE_ID` will exist. That's not always true. We have two projects which share the same database, and they have different values for `settings.SITE_ID`.

When I migrate the first project, all good. When I migrate the second project, there is a `Site` object, so this migration doesn't bother creating a new one to match `settings.SITE_ID`, and then I get `django.contrib.sites.models.DoesNotExist: Site matching query does not exist.`

I think it could be fixed by changing the code like this:

```
def make_site(apps, schema_editor):
    Site = apps.get_model("sites", "Site")
    Site.objects.get_or_create(
        pk=settings.SITE_ID,
        defaults=dict(
            site.name='example',
            site.domain='example.com',
        ))
```

This also applies to https://github.com/edoburu/django-fluent-contents/issues/55

Any objections?

issue_title: Provide CFFI bindings for rdkafka or a Pure Python version for PyPy
issue_body:
cpyext is much slower on PyPy. We need to provide CFFI bindings for librdkafka in order to match the throughput of the CPython extension.
Alternatively we can provide a pure python version and disallow building the extension under PyPy.
This is a question of throughput and when the GIL can be released.
Will C always be faster than PyPy in this case? Are there opportunities to release the GIL before I/O occurs?

issue_title: Enhancements to data types policy and handling
issue_body:
This follows from a lot of discussion in #1224 and on [gitter.im](https://gitter.im/scikit-image/scikit-image) (search for "vmin"). Summary:

- many images represent physical quantities and doing an `img_as_whatever` on it can result in precision loss and/or confusion for the user. 
- we should thus minimize messing with users' data ranges *except where required*
- where it makes sense, we should still assume correct input according to the current data policy. For example, just as rescaling user data before doing e.g. a dilation can be misleading/confusing, so can rescaling the display range, as matplotlib does by default. That's why the mpl plugin for `io.imshow` by default uses the image ranges displayed in our data types.

To do here:

- [ ] Update `doc/source/user_guide/data_types.txt` to say that input data types will be respected wherever possible.
- [x] Update morphology functions to support all dtypes, see #1216, #1074, and #1049.
- [x] Update the MPL `io.imshow` plugin to raise warnings when image range exceeds the expected range *and* when image range is too small to display within the expected range (1/255. of the expected range).

issue_title: KNNClassifier wishlist
issue_body:
This Issue has been broken up into (and replaced by):

https://github.com/numenta/nupic/issues/2271

https://github.com/numenta/nupic/issues/2270

Improvements to KNNClassifier

- [ ] infer() should have options for sparse and dense inputs. Right now, for example, if distance metric is 'rawOverlap' infer must be called with a dense input even if classifier was trained on sparse input repr. If sparse input is used, the classification is non-deterministic
- [ ] Add getPatternCount() accessor method, possible others for a nice API
- [ ] Understand `partitionId` and implement, remove, or document appropriately
- [ ] Understand and then remove or document `rowID`
- [ ] Move `leaveOneOutTest` method from KNNClassifier module to appropriate test module
- [x] Improve explanations of constructor parameters
- [ ] Remove unused arguments from infer() method
- [ ] Move `leaveOneOutTest` to another module
- [ ] SVD / PCA features need better docs
- [ ] reorder parameters in constructor putting dependent parameters after their dependencies


There are several instances of KNN Classifier that one can make by specifying the following constructor arguments and, ideally, we should have adequate test coverage for the different possibilities:
- [ ] useSparseMemory (boolean)

distanceMethod (string)
- [ ] "norm"
- [ ] "rawOverlap"
- [ ] "pctOverlapOfLarger"
- [ ] "pctOverlapOfProto"


Ensure that infer() & learn() respond nicely to:
- [x] Sparse representation with unsorted indices 
- [x] Empty input list

issue_title: AppVeyor create invalid wheel file names for iterative builds
issue_body:
The recently merged PR https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/pull/857 allowed for iterative NuPIC builds to pick up a CORE_COMMITISH SHA labelled nupic.bindings wheel from AWS S3. The addition of the long-SHA into the wheel file name worked in that instance, but has subsequently failed for certain NuPIC AppVeyor builds [1].

The invalid Pip wheel file name error is thrown via this [2] InvalidWheelFilename assert. Pip wheel.py uses a regex to split a wheel file name string into certain variables. With the `ver` regex capture point (`(?P<ver>\d.*?)`) assuming that wheel version strings in the file name start with a digit (`\d.*`).

The AppVeyor yaml script requires a change to make sure that the `ver` part of iterative build wheels starts with a digit before the long-SHA. Proposal is to prepend the BINDINGS_VERSION to the long-SHA here [3], and the wheels subsequent upload to S3 here [4].

An online regex editor, such as https://regex101.com/#python, can be used to validate this required change.

1 https://ci.appveyor.com/project/numenta-ci/nupic/build/0.1.0.1204
2 https://github.com/pypa/pip/blob/develop/pip/wheel.py#L616
3 https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/master/appveyor.yml#L129
4 https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/master/appveyor.yml#L162

issue_title: `"pycapnp==0.5.5"` is not in `bindings/py/requirements.txt`
issue_body:
README.md states:

> The Python dependencies (NumPy and pycapnp) can be installed with pip:
> 
> ```
> pip install -r bindings/py/requirements.txt
> ```

But capnp is not indeed in `bindings/py/requirements.txt`, instead, it's installed by `extra_require` in `setup.py`:

```python
  setup(
    name="nupic.bindings",
    version=getVersion(),
    # This distribution contains platform-specific C++ libraries, but they are not
    # built with distutils. So we must create a dummy Extension object so when we
    # create a binary file it knows to make it platform-specific.
    ext_modules=[Extension('nupic.dummy', sources = ['dummy.c'])], 
    namespace_packages=["nupic"],
    install_requires=findRequirements(platform),
    packages=find_packages(),
    package_data={
        "nupic.proto": ["*.capnp"],
        "nupic.bindings": ["*.so", "*.pyd"],
    },
    extras_require = {"capnp": ["pycapnp==0.5.5"]},
```

Is this intended? Why?

This causes the following 2 problems:

* A new user would fail if only goes "Building from Source -> Prerequisites" to "Developer Installation -> Configure and generate C++ build files" on a whole new environment
* inconsistency between documentation and actual code

issue_title: SP getBoostedOverlaps() returns slightly corrupted values
issue_body:
I fixed this once, but I reverted the fix because I thought "it's not causing any issues" https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/commit/889f7c38d2850b97227cc6ac843c49922c9e9245

Today I was debugging, and I saw that `getBoostedOverlaps()` was often showing different values for columns that had the same overlaps and boost factors. Eventually I realized that it was because of the issue above. So it is causing issues!

We should stop messing with the overlaps vector.

issue_title: RFC: reporting module
issue_body:
The idea for a reporting module come from a couple of use cases that I have for SCOAP3. The main principle would be to have plugin based module for periodical/on event generation of "reports". Reports could be:
- stored (for a future reference) or send to specified email addresses,
- in a different formats: email, PDF, CSV etc.,
- they could be triggered periodically (every day/week etc) or on event (faild bibcheck etc.)

In general it could be simillar to bibcheck/checker but should not change data and scheduling should be done per plugin.

Use cases that I have for SCOAP3:
- reports of status (number of records, and some other queried/dynamically generated statistics) of repository sent to SCOAP3 partners,
- report about ingestion and compliance checks (we check metadata, PDF and publishers website for certain things - this are not only simple checks) created when compliance check fails on ingestion of record to the system or periodically as summary
- ad hoc reports in CSV format for some specific queries metadata.

Any comments/ideas:question:

issue_title: RFC deposit: authors field in article submission form
issue_body: @invenio-developers commented on [Sun Jun 08 2014](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/issues/1774) **Issue by [kneczaj](https://github.com/kneczaj)** _Friday Jun 06, 2014 at 16:03 GMT_ _Originally opened as https://github.com/jirikuncar/invenio/issues/302_ ---- Hello, I just saw that the design of the author field which I introduced in bootstrap migration commit was reverted. In my opinion the previous version (first screenshot) is not ideal because of two things: * move/remove buttons are really small * there is much space wasted between the inline fields My design moves also 'move' button to the left to separate it from 'remove' button as the functionality differs much, and misclicking is not welcome here ;) although this is a minor issue, and for sure will get much discussion so I don't insist on it. I attach both screenshots to discuss on them. Your opinions and other designs are welcome! :) Design 1 "small buttons" (original): ![authors2](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/4245137/3202717/4de649f8-ed93-11e3-8f80-3dd8d28c20d9.png) Design 2 "large buttons" (mine): ![authors](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/4245137/3202636/860ca058-ed92-11e3-8fa7-226829317ebe.png) Design no. 2 is a fast revert and just presents the general idea, it may need tuning the fields sizes and removing some css glitches. --- @lnielsen commented on [Thu Jun 12 2014](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/issues/1774#issuecomment-45847142) Here's a better screenshot of design 1 (it's a bug that the buttons are on top of each other): ![zenodo 2014-06-12 10-50-58](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1698163/3255752/eff67da6-f20e-11e3-8a91-4b949cacbafb.png) The reason for the revert was it was the early days of PU-branch and the change to Design 2 was not discussed with me as the initial author and the only production user of it (Design 2 broke functionality on Zenodo). Additionally we were on a tight deadline so I didn't get to discussed the revert with you as it ought to have been. It's a bit more calm so I'm happy to start a discussion on how we can create an ideal author/affiliation field. I see the button placement only as a minor part of this. The goal of the author/affiliation field is to make it as easy as possible for a submitter to enter high quality metadata. To that end I believe we need: * Auto-complete as much as possible (name and affiliation) from appropriate sources. * Assisted formatting of name if it's not formatted in the pattern Family name, First name. * Obtain author/organization identifiers (such as ORCID) in a easy assisted manner. * Support copy/paste of authors from PDF/HTML pages via parsing clipboard content. I suggest making a survey of other systems to get ideas for how we can best create the author/affiliation field. ### Button placement **Re. design 2:** I don't like the having the buttons as bootstrap input groups (it makes the visually to impactful for me), and I don't like that there's no whitespace in-between the fields. **Re. design 1:** I agree that you are more likely to accidentally hit the close button. In my experience it has however not been an issue. Also, I believe having both buttons together, make it easier for the user to identify the possible features of the author field (ordering/removal). --- @greut commented on [Thu Jun 12 2014](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/issues/1774#issuecomment-45848901) Things to consider: - accessibility: not everyone can perform a drag and drop operation - touch screen: - a big finger cannot click on a small arrow - no `:hover` state or `title` to help the user understanding the UI

issue_title: Normal metaclass resolution not applied to InterfaceClass
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/791218, @lrowe reported:

> While experimenting with my InterfaceClass subclass I noticed that it was only being used when it was specified as the first of the bases. I believe this is because InterfaceClass is not a subclass of ``type``, so the normal metaclass derivation logic is not applied. The branch at svn+ssh://svn.zope.org/repos/main/zope.interface/branches/elro-metaclass-resolution implements that logic in ``InterfaceClass.__new__``, picking from the base metaclasses that metaclass which subclasses all other base metaclasses.
>
> The motivating factor here is: I need an Interface with a hook that gets called after ``InterfaceClass.__init__`` to allow for:
>
> - Checking that field names supplied as tagged values from directives correspond to actual field names to detect typos.
>
> - Registering the ``Interface`` instance for further configuration to be executed by a zope.configuration action.
>
> Relevant changeset: http://zope3.pov.lt/trac/changeset/121497/zope.interface/branches/elro-metaclass-resolution
>
> Example InterfaceClass subclass: http://dev.plone.org/plone/browser/plone.supermodel/branches/elro-directives/plone/supermodel/model.py#L58

issue_title: Add support for swappable token model
Process.start() method does not call Process._notify()
issue_body:
The start() method of the Process class does not call the _notify() method as other methods (i.e. stop()) do.
This prevents the executor to connect back to the agent and start processing events, because the method Process._run() never assigns the field _master and therefore never creates a Connection instance. 
As a result the launched task remains in STAGING state until it fails a couple of minutes after.

A workaround to this issue is to extend the MesosExecutorDriver override the start() method adding a call to _notify().

Is this the intended behavior?

```
* Start the app. Tap "refresh"
* **BUG 1** Tap "fullscreen". Screen will shift so the top is offscree; i.e. pos (0,0) is not shifted down to where the android soft buttons used to be. updating the canvas (by tapping any button, or pressing the "update canvas" button, which explicity does an `ask_update()` will fix this.
* Leave the app by swiping up from the bottom and hitting the circle button.
* relaunch the app
* Note, android soft menu bar has reappeared. App is positioned _under_ the menu bar (**BUG 2**)
*Press "fullscreen". Soft menu will disappear, but note app window is not full-sized (black space at top, resolution wrong) **BUG 3**
* Worse, notice that your touch events on the buttons are offset by the size of the black bar (pos is out of sync with displayed graphics)

This was reproduced on a Google Pixel C tablet with android 6.0.1 (Build MXC89L)

issue_title: Referencing variables declared as arguments
issue_body:
Marvelous project.
But either I'm missing something or for some reason variables which are declared inside the function cannot be referenced directly.


Example:

```
def arneodo(v, a = -5.5, b = 3.5, c = -1.0):
	dv = np.empty(v.shape)	
	dv[:, 0] = v[:, 1]
	dv[:, 1] = v[:, 2]
	dv[:, 2] = ne.evaluate("-a * x - b * y - z + c * x**3", {'a' : a, 'b' : b, 'c' : c, 'x': v[:, 0], 'y': v[:, 1], 'z': v[:, 2]})
	#dv[:, 2] = -a * v[:, 0] - b * v[:, 1] - v[:, 2] + c * v[:, 0]**3
	return dv
```

{'a' : a, 'b' : b, 'c' : c,   should be redundant but won't work without it.

issue_title: NaN for the start date
issue_body:
Hello,
I just experienced a strange behaviour with some European Equities: 
`
import pandas_datareader.data as web
start='2017-05-25'
end='2017-10-01'

f=web.DataReader('ASML.AS', 'yahoo', start=start, end=end)
`

The first line of the resulting DF is 2017-05-24 and it has NaN for all attributes (open, high, low close etc.). 
If I move the start date back another day, the data for 2017-05-24 is included, but the day before then has the NaNs.

On Yahoo Finance, the historical data seems to be complete for all days including 2017-05-24:
https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ASML.AS/history?p=ASML.AS

I am not sure what's causing this. Any feedback would be appreciated!

(python 3.6.2, pandas-datareader 0.5.0)

issue_title: Add an IPython Widget?
issue_body:
I created a [QGrid IPython Widget](https://gist.github.com/blink1073/184601e653a0e4aace9f) (valid for IPython 3.x).  It adds the ability to edit the dataframe in-place, including adding and removing rows.  It requires no changes to the existing codebase.

Are you interested in a PR that adds this widget?  
I'm not sure what the best way to package it would be.   Perhaps a `qgrid.prepare_notebook()` that loads `qgrid_widget.js` using `IPython.display.Javascript`.  You could then expose `qgrid.QGridWidget` as well as `qgrid.edit_dataframe()`, that runs the application with the add/remove row buttons.

![qgrid_widget](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2096628/9028146/3611c7e2-3934-11e5-97b0-131a3e5be2f2.gif)

issue_title: Fama-French data is never read from cache
issue_body:
In `utils.load_portfolio_risk_factors` there is a call to `get_returns_cached(filepath, get_fama_french, end)`.
However, the FamaFrench data only goes up to Sept 30, so if `end>"Sept 30"`, then the check `returns.index[-1] < latest_dt` in `utils.get_returns_cached` returns false, and the data is downloaded anew.  In tests, this is the most time-consuming part of generating a tear sheet.

issue_title: Return outputs of compute functions
issue_body:
Currently, pyfolio takes inputs, feeds them into the `compute` functions, plots the output, and then (usually) throws away the results of the `compute` functions. This is undesirable, both for Q internal  use and open-source use.

Ideally, we would like `create_x_tear_sheet` to return an object that contains all the results of pyfolio analysis. E.g. a `round_trips_analysis` object/dict that I can query to find all numerical output or features. This will make pyfolio much more useful for algo analysis.

issue_title: Mark required fields as required in the help text
issue_body:
As an observation about usability, it's hard to use the help to build a complete command because it's tricky to know which fields are required. For instance, if you try to create a `job_template` without all the required fields:

```bash
$ tower-cli job_template create --name="deploy cool web app"
Error: Missing required fields: inventory, project, playbook, credential
```

Those are a lot of fields, but typing:

```
$ tower-cli job_template create --help
```

gives no indication about which fields are required.

My humble suggestion is to make a change along the lines of the following, added to `base.py`.

```python
                        # Apply the option to the method.
                        option_help = field.help
                        if field.required:
                            option_help = '[required] ' + option_help
                        click.option(
                            *args,
                            default=field.default if not ignore_defaults
                            else None,
                            help=option_help,
                            type=field.type,
                            show_default=field.show_default
                        )(new_method)
```

This will add the text "[required]" to the start of all help text messages for required fields. For example, in the case of a job template, the help text will read like the following:

    Options:
      --name TEXT                     [required] The name field.
      --description TEXT              The description field.
      --job-type [run|check]          [required] The job_type field.  [default:
                                      run]
      --inventory INVENTORY           [required] The inventory field.
      --project PROJECT               [required] The project field.
      --playbook TEXT                 [required] The playbook field.
      --machine-credential CREDENTIAL
                                      [required] The machine_credential field.
      --cloud-credential CREDENTIAL   The cloud_credential field.
      --forks INTEGER                 The forks field.
      --limit TEXT                    The limit field.

Clearly this isn't difficult to implement, but the question is whether this is a valid improvement or just more terminal clutter. We also need to write tests to for the change. I also would like to get some feedback about how to format the text. Should it be capitalized, like "[REQUIRED]"? Should it go in a different place, like at the end of the text? Maybe it would be better to just list the required fields at the very start of the help text (similar to the existing "missing required fields" error)?

issue_title: Skipped Parameter Validation
issue_body:
In the _validation.py_ file there is a _ParameterValidator_ class with follow line in it's init method:
```python
self.parameters = {k: list(g) for k, g in itertools.groupby(parameters, key=lambda p: p['in'])}
```

Here `parameters` is a list of parameters for an endpoint from the swagger specification. The expected behaviour is that the parameters get sorted into a dictionary based on their type. But the _groupby_ has a caveat which is mentioned in the documentation.

>  The operation of groupby() is similar to the `uniq` filter in Unix. It generates a break or new group every time the value of the key function changes (which is why it is usually necessary to have sorted the data using the same key function). That behavior differs from SQL’s GROUP BY which aggregates common elements regardless of their input order.

When the parameters are sorted there is no issue. However when the parameters are mixed when the groupby() function is run it results in:

```python
('query', <itertools._grouper object at 0x10c6a4690>)
('path', <itertools._grouper object at 0x10c6a4a10>)
('query', <itertools._grouper object at 0x10c6a4690>)
```

So the first set of query parameters are overwritten by the second set of parameters. This means that the first set of parameters are not validated at all.

I can submit a PR with a fix in two ways. Either sort the parameters like so:
```python
self.parameters = {k: list(g) for k, g in itertools.groupby(parameters.sort(key=lambda p:p['in']), key=lambda p: p['in'])}
```
Or create a dictionary like so:
```python
self.parameters = defaultdict(list)
for p in parameters:
    self.parameters[p['in]].append(p)
```

issue_title: [gtk3] Binding of show/hide hotkey fails on Wayland
issue_labels: [{'id': 499874709, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw0OTk4NzQ3MDk=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/Guake/guake/labels/Epic:%20GTK%203%20port', 'name': 'Epic: GTK 3 port', 'color': '000000', 'default': False}, {'id': 80727024, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw4MDcyNzAyNA==', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/Guake/guake/labels/Priority:%20High', 'name': 'Priority: High', 'color': 'f7c6c7', 'default': False}, {'id': 5048088, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw1MDQ4MDg4', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/Guake/guake/labels/Type:%20Feature%20Request', 'name': 'Type: Feature Request', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
I just upgraded to version 3.0.2 of guake which uses GTK3 instead of GTK2. As a result of this guake runs with full Wayland support. With Wayland there is no way for an application to register a global hotkey like it's possible with X11. My current workaround to get the new version of guake running under Wayland is to set the following environement variable `GDK_BACKEND=x11` for the guake process. With this guake 3 will use also XWayland like it was the case for guake 2. So for now it's working but I think the better approach to solve this issue is to create a GAction which would appear in the global hotkeys section of the compositor. See [this issus](https://github.com/kupferlauncher/keybinder/issues/11#issuecomment-333390043) for more information.

Edit: I just figured out that the workaround with the `GDK_BACKEND` environment variable isn't the best. In this case the show/hide hotkey works only if the window that has currently the focus is also rendered by XWayland. So I looked for a better workaround and found the command line flag `--toggle-visibility`. So I configured a global hotkey in my compositor that calls `guake --toggle-visibility`. With this there is no need to run guake using the x11 backend. But to get this working the changes of the following commit 0f2da79c48cee706d389413a7e9c7ae715cb7ef6 are required which aren't yet contained in a release.

issue_title: normalize column names client-side instead of looking at the database
issue_body:
This can easily be done with a function like this:

```python
def norm_column(colname):
    """Convert a valid pandas DataFrame column name into a CARTO-supported
    PostgreSQL name"""
    normed = re.sub('[^a-z_0-9]', '_', colname.lower())
    if re.match('^[0-9]', normed):
        return '_' + normed
    return normed
```

Another option is to have all the column names on the database as quoted, but that seems like it would cause more problems than normalizing.

issue_title: Use sdl linking for mkl
issue_body:
I noticed that dynet links directly against `mkl_intel_lp64 mkl_intel_thread mkl_core`, which somehow doesn't work with my use case (i.e. using the scala bindings and mkl).

I get the same error as described [in this intel forum post](https://software.intel.com/en-us/forums/intel-math-kernel-library/topic/733207) and the answer is to instead link against `mkl_rt`.

Linking against `mkl_rt` uses the [single dynamic library approach](https://software.intel.com/en-us/articles/a-new-linking-model-single-dynamic-library-mkl_rt-since-intel-mkl-103/), in which this library decides at runtime which "backend library" to use.

Changing this linking behavior can be achieved by switching the libraries in `CMakeLists.txt` lines 45 and 47.  I did this locally and it solves my issue.  However, I'm not sure enough of other implications to create a pull request directly.

issue_title: BigTable: row_data _validateChunk performance
issue_body:
Looking at the performance graph for reading a large number (1000) of rows, each row having 10 cells in one column family, the validation of chunks takes a large percentage of time (>25%), as measured by cProfile.  Areas for tuning are identified below.

**Validation of timestamp_micros ordering** 
While a row is being read, a validation is made to make sure that cells for one qualifier are in timestamp_micros order (most recent first):
```
    def _validate_chunk_row_in_progress(self, chunk):
        ...
        previous = self._previous_cell
        _raise_if(self._same_as_previous(chunk) and
                  chunk.timestamp_micros <= previous.timestamp_micros)

    def _same_as_previous(self, chunk):
        previous = self._previous_cell
        return (chunk.row_key == previous.row_key and
                chunk.family_name == previous.family_name and
                chunk.qualifier == previous.qualifier and
                chunk.labels == previous.labels)
```
This validation may be extraneous because:

1. A RowFilter may have been applied - CellsColumnLimitFilter(1) - which will limit the result to the cell with the most recent timestamp.
2. There may only be one cell value for the qualifier.
3. The BigTable server may ALWAYS be providing the cells in timestamp order.

Two possible tunings:

1. Remove the validation if the condition is unlikely to occur in the ReadRowResponse.
2. If the validation is kept, it could be performed only when qualifier cell lists with length > 1 are detected AFTER the chunks for the qualifier are consumed.

issue_title: Storage: app_identity_service.GetAccessToken NOT_ALLOWED
issue_body:
- GAE standard env
- google-cloud-storage==1.9.0

steps to reproduce:

- create a long-lived Bucket object
- do some file operations, under the hood it will get an access_token from IdentityService for you

code example: https://github.com/zyzniewski/app_identity_service_bug/blob/master/main.py

issue_title: Datastore: Return values from delete()
issue_body:
I love Google Cloud Datastore for Python, after  7-8 hours of fiddling around with it, it finally works as I want it to. However one quick thing that could make life easier is if there was a return flag from functions (such as client.delete(key)) as front end could save a lot of time (both during execution and while developing). It seems to return, well, nothing at the mean time unlike client.get(key) which has a return that can be used as a simple flag. Serverless architecture is awesome but latency can be a real issue (although Google is pretty good, it is still noticable). Every line of code matters (efficieny-wise) and overall I feel more returns would allow frontend developers more tools while decreasing overall server pokes. Sorry if this should not go here by the way.

Cheers.

issue_title: Use more meaningful log message than "Finalize cipher failed"
issue_body:
I'm switching to AEAD cipher, when I discovered the [Ban Brute Force Crackers](/shadowsocks/shadowsocks/wiki/Ban-Brute-Force-Crackers) feature, and also the [fail2ban config](/shadowsocks/shadowsocks/blob/master/utils/fail2ban/shadowsocks.conf) (alternative to the banning script, see #517) are rendered useless, because only a general "Finalize cipher failed" message is logged.

I've traced the message to the following code: [mbedtls.py:299](/shadowsocks/shadowsocks/blob/e332ec93e9c90f1cbee676b022bf2c5d5b7b1239/shadowsocks/crypto/mbedtls.py#L299) but I'm not sure how can I change it to achieve the same result as before (when using non-AEAD ciphers in [v2.8.2](https://pypi.org/project/shadowsocks/2.8.2/)).

### Expected behavior
When an invalid login was attempted, `ssserver` log the following message to log file:
```
2016-07-17 18:05:03 ERROR can not parse header when handling connection from 112.10.137.6:20463
```
(taken from https://atjason.com/IT/ss_fail2ban.html)

### Current behavior
When an invalid login was attempted, `ssserver` log the following message to log file:
```
2018-06-30 00:00:00 ERROR    Finalize cipher failed
```

@yzou, @mengskysama for issue from #803

(I asked this on StackOverflow but I think there is a better chance of response here)

I would like to pass the Elastic Load Balancer `ConnectionSettings` attribute only if it's set in my configuration, otherwise I don't want to specify it.

If I set it to default python `None` then I get an error about the value not being of the expected type of `troposphere.elasticloadbalancing.ConnectionSettings`.

issue_title: Distributing multiversion packages in a pex file
issue_body:
Thank you so much for creating this great project! 

I am the author of the sphinxserve package that uses pex to significantly simplify distribution. Pex does an awesome job allowing to distribute pure python packages and binary ones for the same python minor versions (eg: 3.4.x) within a unique pex file. As Python 3 gets more adopted, very soon well have systems running 2.7, 3.4, 3.5 and 3.6, each of them potentially requiring different package name dependencies.

I am using the following to create pex files:
    pex -v --disable-cache --no-index -f dist -c sphinxserve -o pex/scripts/sphinxserve dist/*

My attempts to create a unique pex containing different python version packages were unsuccessful. As soon as 3.4 binary wheels are added (onto dist containing 3.5 wheels), the following happens::

Could not satisfy all requirements for WheelPackage('file:///Volumes/data/prog/sphinxserve/dist/MarkupSafe-0.23-cp34-cp34m-macosx_10_6_intel.whl'):

issue_title: Support processing dependency links?
issue_body: We specify dependency links for internal packages in many of our `setup.py`s. We can force pip to follow them with the `PIP_PROCESS_DEPENDENCY_LINKS=1` environment variable, but PEX doesn't use that. It would be great if we could do similar for PEX, but in the meantime we can use `--index-url` or `--find-links`.

issue_title: PrepCommand targets should accept multiple goals
issue_body:
Currently [only 1 goal](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/build_graph/prep_command.py#L44) can be specified leading to copy/paste boilerplate when its desired to have the `PrepCommand` run prior to several goals.

For example, I'm having to do this over in Aurora:
```python
# build-support/thrift/BUILD
prep_command(
  name='prepare_binary_compile',
  goal='compile',
  prep_environ=False,
  prep_executable='build-support/thrift/prepare_binary.sh',
)

prep_command(
  name='prepare_binary_binary',
  goal='binary',
  prep_environ=False,
  prep_executable='build-support/thrift/prepare_binary.sh',
)

prep_command(
  name='prepare_binary_test',
  goal='test',
  prep_environ=False,
  prep_executable='build-support/thrift/prepare_binary.sh',
)

target(
  name='prepare_binary',
  dependencies=[
    ':prepare_binary_compile',
    ':prepare_binary_binary',
    ':prepare_binary_test',
  ],
)
```

And then add the `build-support/thrift:prepare_binary` to thrift targets.

issue_title: Cache ./pants bootstrapping in CI using stages
issue_body: As @wisechengyi noted in [@pants-devel](https://groups.google.com/d/topic/pants-devel/_vEJ0F_190U/discussion), CI now spends ~10 minutes per shard bootstrapping pants. This time is largely spent compiling the native engine and so the time will only grow as more of Pants moves to Rust. As a stop-gap to Pants growing and then dogfooding Rust support such that we could use Pants own distributed cache (seems like this might be doable someday), leverage Travis-CI build stages to 1st build a pants pex, upload it to S3 and then use that pex in parallel on test shards. An additional benefit to this setup is that we can have a fully vetted pants pex as a release target instead of a pex built after-the-fact (https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/6014).

issue_title: Protect restful cache from malicious PUT  requests
issue_body:
I've configured our distributed cache with an nginx server that accepts http.  After a discussion with our security team, we would like to do something to protect the cache server from malicious PUT requests.  A couple of solutions came up:

- Use https for uploads and insist on a client cert that matches a whitelist.
- Add a way to sign artifacts before uploading so that a client can verify that artifacts were created from a trusted source
- Create a separate port for uploading and firewall off a separate upload port and only allow certain hosts to upload.  (Actually, I just made this up, I'm not sure if our security team would go for this. It would be a big headache to maintain the list of allowed hosts as we use a dynamic pool of machines that would be legitimate sources of build artifacts.)

issue_title: Multiproc files not cleaned up if worker gets segfault/oom/...
issue_body:
In multiproc mode, if one of the gunicorn workers gets killed because of a segfault or out-of-memory situation, it doesn't get a chance to clean up its `.db` files. These files are thus left in the multiproc folder, distorting the metrics.

Maybe this can be solved by moving the cleanup hook into the gunicorn "master" process. My suggestion is to solve this in the Python client instead, by deleting all `.db` files with dead PIDs before aggregating the metrics.

issue_title: README Examples Don't Work
issue_body:
Two README examples fail. One:
```python
from plaid import Client
from plaid import errors as plaid_errors
from plaid.utils import json


client = Client()
institutions = json.loads(client.institutions().content)
categories = json.loads(client.categories().content)
```
And it breaks cause the Client doesn't actually allow no params for `client_id` and `secret`. Even if I use a Client with a legitimate params, as below it will fail because of `json.loads` (see below).

I also tried this example (with my actual client, username, password, etc):

```python
from plaid import Client
from plaid import errors as plaid_errors
from plaid.utils import json


client = Client(client_id='***', secret='***')
account_type = 'bofa'

try:
    response = client.connect(account_type, {
     'username': '***',
     'password': '***'
    })
except plaid_errors.PlaidError:
     pass
else:
    connect_data = json.loads(response.content)
``` 

This works with a 200 but `response.content` is a byte object and not a string. So json.loads fails. This happens using both python's json library and plaid's json util. `response.text` and `response.json()` still give me what I want so there's a workaround.

I'm using python 3.4 and it looks like you guys added support for that in the 1.10.

issue_title: File sizes/resources for granular import/exports are overestimated
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

1. In Export mode, selecting a topic will say _every_ resource under topic is exported
1. In RemoteImport mode, selecting a topic will say_every_ resource under topic is imported
1. In Local Import mode, selecting a topic will say_every_ resource under topic is imported

And the file sizes reflect those high resource counts
### Expected behavior

1. In Export mode, selecting a topic will say only resources that are _on the exporting device_ are exported
1. In RemoteImport mode, selecting a topic will say resources _not yet on the importing device_ are imported
1. In Local Import mode, selecting a topic will say resources _not yet on the importing device & on the importing device_ are imported. (Due to technical limitations, we do not know how many Resources are on the importing device, for an arbitrary topic).

### User-facing consequences

1. Incorrect estimates
1. User being blocked from completing transfer if system thinks transfer goes over capacity

issue_title: <Imp Enhancement> Learner username is not displayed on the top right corner of the browser on a Portrait mode in a tablet.
issue_body:
## Summary
Learner username is not displayed on the top right corner of the browser on a Portrait mode in a tablet.

When the tablet is key in landscape mode the learners can see their logged in username on the top right corner, but when you switch it to portrait mode learner's login ID is not visible and on top of that he/she cannot know who's ID he is logged in, as the username is not visible on the dropdown option on the top right corner.

**Important user story**
Its always good to have the learners name displayed all times at the top right corner, so that the learner is aware that he/she is using their own account. This is a big issue since we have't implemented the #2131 and because of this at-least 50% of the leaner end up progressing on the wrong student account. Some learners prefer using Portrait mode and some prefer Landscape mode, hence is enhancement is important.

## System information
 - Version: 0.4.5 and 0.5.0
 - Operating system: Ubuntu 14.04LTS
 - Browser: Chrome.

## How to reproduce
1. Login in a tablet on Landscape mode, notice the user name is clearly visible at the top right corner.

issue_title: MarathonHealthCheck class doesn't support 0.8.2
issue_body:
Marathon 0.8.2 added a new http health check option called `ignoreHttp1xx`. Calling `list_apps` or `get_app` for an http app raises the following exception.

```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/marathon/client.py", line 135, in list_apps
    apps = self._parse_response(response, MarathonApp, is_list=True, resource_name='apps')
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/marathon/client.py", line 50, in _parse_response
    return [clazz.from_json(resource) for resource in target]
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/marathon/models/base.py", line 30, in from_json
    return cls(**{to_snake_case(k): v for k,v in attributes.iteritems()})
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/marathon/models/app.py", line 101, in __init__
    for hc in (health_checks or [])
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/marathon/models/base.py", line 30, in from_json
    return cls(**{to_snake_case(k): v for k,v in attributes.iteritems()})
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'ignore_http1xx'
```

This fix to support an additional arg in MarathonHealthCheck is easy and I'll do a PR shortly.

*The main question is, is there another way to get this right w/o having to add this argument?*

- [Marathon v0.8.2 release notes](https://github.com/mesosphere/marathon/releases/tag/v0.8.2)
- [Marathon Health Checks](https://mesosphere.github.io/marathon/docs/health-checks.html)

issue_title: Compatibility breaks
issue_body:
I've been working on a codebase written against 0.6.1. I'm updating to use the latest (0.7.3) and have run into a few compatibility breaks that I'm documenting here. I'll add to this list as I complete my upgrade.

- Connection.connect no longer accepts positional arguments

The previous usage was:
```
Controller.connect(endpoint, user, secret, cacert)

Controller.connect(endpoint=endpoint, username=user, password=secret, cacert=cacert)

issue_title: Error in creating a DC with unconnected volumes
issue_body:
If you are trying to create a datacenter with volumes, which are not connected to a virtual machine, you will get an error message like:

`ERROR - Exception: (400, u"Failed to parse request body. [line: 1, column: 98] Unrecognized field 'image_password'")`

The reason is, that the Volume objects are not mangled with self._create_volume_dict() like in other methods, like create_volume().

issue_title: Provide a feed dict as an alternative syntax for specifying inputs
issue_body: When working with published graphs, it is not recommended to change the name of the input. Instead of working by convention, one should be able to specify a feed dictionary that associates the name of a tensor in the graph to the name of a column in the dataframe.

issue_title: TableCPD type checking too strict
issue_body:
TableCPD init method requires variable_card to be an int. This makes it incompatible with model.get_cardinality(.), which returns a numpy.int64

The fix is trivial, replace

`if not isinstance(variable_card, int):`

with 
`import numbers`
`if not isinstance(variable_card,numbers.Integral)`

should I create a pull request?

issue_title: Limit to compatible Django versions with setup.py's install_requires
issue_body:
As per https://packaging.python.org/requirements/#install-requires , CleanerVersion's setup.py should limit installation to known compatible Django versions.

If this has the consequence, though, of downloading a different Django version than the one that a user already has installed, it is not an optimal solution.

Two alternatives (which should perhaps be used together):
  * check early in setup.py to see if a non-compatible version is already installed, and error out if that is the case (like in https://trac-hacks.org/ticket/9800#comment:4 ).
  * have a runtime check in the top level of versions/models.py, that raises an error if an incompatible version of Django is installed.

issue_title: Release v0.4
issue_body:
We are heading for a big release, containing:

- Refactor of locate to make it more modular. Some API breaks. (PR 400, 406
- Better performance of gray dilation (PR 361)
- Fix compute drift if dataframes are not sorted (PR 409)
- New refinement using least squares optimization (PR 407)
- New linker that combines gray dilation and linking `FindLinker` (PR 407, 410, 411, 416, 428)
- Refactor of the linking code to give the `Linker` and `FindLinker` a common code base and better API (PR 416)
- Fix double counting of non-linking penalty in non-numba linkers (PR 430)
- Fix the `N` column for `emsd` (PR 434)

On the milestone list are the following:

- [x] Make `locate` even more modular #257
- [x] Adjust default value of `smoothing_size` to the Crocker Grier value #401
- [x] Finish the refactor of the linking code, breaking a part of the linking API (building on #416)
- [ ] Supply a parallelized `batch` function #304 
- [ ] Investigate skimage's moment computations #265 
- [ ] Investigate normalization questions raised in #239
- [ ] Read/write OpenPTV files #243 

I think we should finish the refactor of the linking code, better to change the API in one release. But for the rest, we could postpone until a later release. Thoughts? @danielballan @nkeim @tacaswell

issue_title: Should we release a 0.2.5 to better support latest deps?
issue_body:
On numpy 1.9, the test suite passes locally, but it issues so many warnings (> 4MB of logs!) that [Travis declares it broken](https://api.travis-ci.org/jobs/47066968/log.txt?deansi=true).

Worse, I discovered a problem with numba 0.16 after all. Trackpy works with python 2.7 + numba 0.16 -- and on all platforms, as mentioned in #135. But it fails badly with python 3.4 + numba 0.16. I did not see that one coming.

I don't expect we'll be tagging 0.3 very soon, so I think I will try to address these issues and cherry-pick commits for a 0.2.5 release. To be clear, that release will not include the other changes to the code currently in master. Good idea?

issue_title: v1/v2 40 needs better stats handling
Constraints only considered when there's an equal to symbol included
issue_body:
Consider "x" an integer ranging between 0 to 10.
Objective is: x + 10
Problem is: Minimize
Constraint: x > 4.1
Expected Result: x = 5, obj = 15
Obtained Result: x = 0, obj = 10
Code:
```python
from pulp import *
prob = LpProblem("test1", LpMinimize)
x = LpVariable("x", 0, 10, 'Integer')
prob += x + 10, "obj"
prob += x > 4.1, "c1"
prob.solve()
print("Status:", LpStatus[prob.status])
print(prob.variables()[0].name, "=", prob.variables()[0].varValue)
print("objective=", value(prob.objective))
```
I've observed that by changing the constraint from ">" to ">=" and it worked as expected.

Code:
```python
from pulp import *
prob = LpProblem("test1", LpMinimize)
x = LpVariable("x", 0, 10, 'Integer')
prob += x + 10, "obj"
prob += x >= 4.1, "c1"   #Changed the constraint from > to >=
prob.solve()
print("Status:", LpStatus[prob.status])
print(prob.variables()[0].name, "=", prob.variables()[0].varValue)
print("objective=", value(prob.objective))
```
Obtained Result: x = 5, obj = 15

issue_title: Trinity: Need a transparent way to run services in their own process.
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The problem statement:  Our current model for exposing APIs across processes is very verbose, requiring a lot of boilerplate.  The overhead for supporting an API across a subprocess boundary is high.  High overhead like that isn't sustainable if we're going to expose a lot of cross process APIs which I think we need.

It needs to be lower, ideally much lower.  We don't need 100% deep access to everything across service boundaries, but we do need it to be easier to expose access to APIs in a sustainable way.

### How can it be fixed

I'm not sure, but I have ideas. (see comments)

issue_title: Conda plugins and argparse
issue_body:
I had posted [this query](https://groups.google.com/a/continuum.io/forum/#!topic/conda/wsf_L_MtwOw) on the mailing list a while ago, and was asked by @msarahan to start a discussion here.

As of conda v4.4.0beta1:
```
(root) [root@a14d37a9b31f ~]# conda-<lets_hit_tab>
conda-build        conda-develop      conda-index        conda-metapackage  conda-skeleton     
conda-convert      conda-env          conda-inspect      conda-render       conda-verify       
```
Now, IIUC, typing `conda<space>{any_subcommand_from_above}` will execute that file and the code path for this is at [conda/cli/conda_argparse.py](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/4.4.0beta1/conda/cli/conda_argparse.py#L46-L71), inside the function: `ArgumentParser.error`

So, is it safe to assume that if I create an executable by the name conda-$something and make sure that it is present in `/path/to/conda/activated/env/bin/`, this functionality will be supported in the future, thereby enabling me to type:
```
(root) [root@a14d37a9b31f ~]# conda<space>$something
```
If this functionality is guaranteed to exist in the future, this makes creating custom sub-commands (plugins?) for conda very easy. One will just have to create a conda package and make sure it is installed in the activated environment.

issue_title: Conda is mixing 32-bit and 64-bit packages (platform is not stored in the conda environment)
issue_body:
There seems to be an inconsistency about the usage of CONDA_FORCE_32BIT.
This is in Windows, look at this sequence of commands:

```
$ set CONDA_FORCE_32BIT=1
$ conda create -n py32 python=2
$ activate py32
$ conda info | grep platform
             platform : win-32

$ deactivate py32
$ set CONDA_FORCE_32BIT=
$ activate py32
$ conda info | grep platform
             platform : win-64

$ python -c "import struct; print(8*struct.calcsize('P'))"
32

$ conda install numpy
$ python -c "import numpy"
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  <...>
    from . import multiarray
ImportError: DLL load failed: %1 is not a valid Win32 application.
```

As you can see, it is currently brittle: if I forget this environment variable (this may be common if I have multiple environments, some in 64-bit and some in 32-bit), then conda will silently mix 64-bit packages with 32-bit packages.

It seems that CONDA_FORCE_32BIT should only be used at _environment creation_, and ignored on activation.

issue_title: `conda update conda` wants to downgrade everything, beyond current version
issue_body:
```
% conda info
Current conda install:

               platform : osx-64
          conda version : 4.2.7
       conda is private : False
      conda-env version : 4.2.7
    conda-build version : 1.21.3
         python version : 2.7.12.final.0
       requests version : 2.10.0
       root environment : /Users/*/anaconda  (writable)
    default environment : /Users/*/anaconda
       envs directories : /Users/*/anaconda/envs
          package cache : /Users/*/anaconda/pkgs
           channel URLs : https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/osx-64/
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/noarch/
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/osx-64/
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/noarch/
            config file : None
           offline mode : False
```
I went to do `conda upgrade conda` to check for updates, when I was greeted with way to much stuff to downgrade. What struck me as weird was how far back it wanted to go for many things. Ex:

```
    scipy:              0.17.1-np111py27_1 --> 0.15.1-np19py27_0
    numpy:              1.11.1-py27_0      --> 1.9.2-py27_0     
    ipython:            5.1.0-py27_0       --> 3.2.0-py27_0     
```

https://docs.continuum.io/anaconda/pkg-docs says spicy is at 0.17.1, and bumpy is at 1.11.1, so I don't know why it wants to pull everything so far back.

issue_title: Ctrl+C does not kill gcc
issue_body:
@sscherfke commented on [Fri Jan 27 2017](https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1705)

If I build a C-lib with conda-build, I can no longer cancel the build using `Ctrl-C`. Conda itself will die, but the compiler processes will continue to run. It looks like the signals are not (or no longer) forwared to all sub processes.

```
$ conda info
Current conda install:

               platform : linux-64
          conda version : 4.3.8
       conda is private : False
      conda-env version : 4.3.8
    conda-build version : 2.1.2
         python version : 3.6.0.final.0
       requests version : 2.12.5
             user-agent : conda/4.3.8 requests/2.12.5 CPython/3.6.0 Linux/4.8.15-200.fc24.x86_64 Fedora/24 glibc/2.23
```

---

@msarahan commented on [Fri Jan 27 2017](https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1705#issuecomment-275667441)

Confirmed.  Switching to conda 4.2.x fixes it for me.  Leaving this issue here until I can isolate it further.

    for var in pysam.VariantFile("my.vcf"):

issue_title: [Enhancement] Add options for own preparsers
issue_body:
OCIDE calls cobc for compilation and conditional dbpre/esqlOC for specific files.
I'd like to see a more general approach being able to set up different preparsers (or even compilers) for specific file types.

Theses are the parts to consider:

* [optional, but nice] parser (to "know" about special sections/paragraphs that don't look like sections/paragraphs, and in the case I'd like to configure these have ";" in column 7 which may lead to not parse the files correctly) - can this part be "inserted" at runtime without the need to recompile the application at all?
* preferences - should be easily doable if the current SQL COBOL preference is changed to "external compilers" and has a list to choose the compilers, "hiding" the current text and file selection list until an entry is added/edited. the default would be only one entry for SQL COBOL (see below)


    Name: SQL COBOL
    Associated extensions: .sqb
    Compiler path: [empty, has to be filled with esql binary)
    Compiler flags: 
    [Checkbox] Only Preparser           Output file [only active if preparser is checked]:  [whatever you do currently]

If "Only Preparser" is checked the output file would be passed to cobc, if not the compiler either does the compilation on it's own or it calls cobc itself.

issue_title: [Documentation] needs an update
issue_body:
Minor issue: launchpad has no repository configured: https://launchpad.net/cobcide/4.0 "No revision control details recorded for OpenCobolIDE 4.0 series. ", please add the repository link there if possible.

The 4.x series has the following description:

>Rewrite of the application and move cobol editor to pyqode.cobol
>The default ui will be the same as in 2.x (with menu bar and toolbars) but with the possiblity to switch to the v3.0 ui by double clicking an editor tab

Sounds misleading :-)


For the rest I reference the online docs at http://opencobolide.readthedocs.org/en/latest/

* "OpenCobol/Gnu Cobol" --> "OpenCOBOL / GnuCOBOL" (the spelling is likely the same on other pages, too)
* Instructions on where and how to install **pyQode**
* http://opencobolide.readthedocs.org/en/latest/whats_new.html#id6 has 4.6.1 as a group item (see left) for the older versions. BTW: having group items for 4.5, 4.6, ... sounds good
* the preferences window changed in the last versions - and it may be a good idea to add a screenshot of the compiler pane from a Windows system
* FAQ: OCIDE works fine with Windows 10, too :-)

issue_title: Better protection against typo in curator config file
issue_body:
I am a new user to curator and with the latest version of curator (4.0.0) I started with the following action config:

```
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_indices
    fitlers:
    - filtertype: pattern
      kind: prefix
      value: logstash-
    - filtertype: age
      source: name
      timestring: '%Y.%m.%d'
      direction: older
      unit: days
      unit_count: 5
```

Instead of 'filters' i typed in 'fitlers', which is a fairly common error to make.

As a result, all my indices in elastics are removed. And it took me some time to figure out why this happened.

I would suggest that curator provide better protection against this sort of disaster. For example, when unrecognized options are encountered, an error shall be raised.

As in SQL, a statement like DELETE FROM table_name WHRER 1=1 shall not delete any thing.

issue_title: cli_ctx should be available for functions in parallel
issue_body:
With the move to the Knack framework it's not possible to access cmd / cli_ctx from within functions called in parallel (like with python multiprocess method). The reason is most likely that these objects cannot be pickled and hence can't be transferred to the target functions.
This causes issues when wanting to report progress (for example).

We might need to think of a way for the cli_ctx to be available in these situations.

---

### Environment summary

Install Method (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, edge build) / CLI version (`az --version`) / OS version / Shell Type (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows)  

```
```

issue_title: Esri cacher doesn't handle 'join' function correctly
issue_body:
The Esri cacher [tries to figure out which fields to request](https://github.com/openaddresses/machine/blob/master/openaddr/cache.py#L330) by inspecting the `conform` object of the source. When it encounters a `dict`, it [looks for the `field` attribute](https://github.com/openaddresses/machine/blob/master/openaddr/cache.py#L321) and adds that to the list of columns to request. In the case of the `join` function, which specifies the name should be `fields`, we end up with a `None` in the list of fields and this function fails.

The fix is to add `field` or `fields`.

Related to this error: https://s3.amazonaws.com/data.openaddresses.io/runs/133223/output.txt

Hi everyone,

I installed Gazebo8 via `brew`, and Bullet is installed along as a [dependency](https://github.com/osrf/homebrew-simulation/blob/master/gazebo8.rb#L41) with [`--with-shared`](https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/master/Formula/bullet.rb#L36) and [`--with-double-precision`](https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/master/Formula/bullet.rb#L45) parameters.

The whole installation process runs out smoothly without errors.
However, running `gazebo`, the following error is raised:
~~~bash
dyld: Library not loaded: @rpath/libBulletDynamics.2.85.dylib
  Referenced from: /usr/local/opt/bullet/lib/libBulletSoftBody.2.85.dylib
  Reason: image not found
Abort trap: 6
~~~

Running `otool -L /usr/local/opt/bullet/lib/libBulletSoftBody.2.85.dylib` reports
~~~bash
/usr/local/opt/bullet/lib/libBulletSoftBody.2.85.dylib:

	@rpath/libBulletCollision.2.85.dylib (compatibility version 2.85.0, current version 2.85.0)
	@rpath/libLinearMath.2.85.dylib (compatibility version 2.85.0, current version 2.85.0)
	/usr/lib/libc++.1.dylib (compatibility version 1.0.0, current version 307.4.0)
	/usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib (compatibility version 1.0.0, current version 1238.0.0)
~~~
while running `otool -l /usr/local/opt/bullet/lib/libBulletSoftBody.2.85.dylib | grep -i rpath` reports
~~~bash
         name @rpath/libBulletDynamics.2.85.dylib (offset 24)
         name @rpath/libBulletCollision.2.85.dylib (offset 24)
         name @rpath/libLinearMath.2.85.dylib (offset 24)
~~~

With the latter command, it seems that `cmd LC_RPATH` load commands are missing.

I was able to workaround this problem by setting
~~~bash
export DYLD_FALLBACK_LIBRARY_PATH=$DYLD_FALLBACK_LIBRARY_PATH:/usr/local/opt/bullet/lib
~~~

@app.route('/post/<int:post_id>')

so, i build a BaseApi class to change the request method:
```
import functools

from aiohttp import web
from aiohttp import hdrs


def _embed_params(func):
    """
    embed params from match_info
    """
    @functools.wraps(func)
    def new_func(self, *args, **kwargs):
        kwargs.update(self.request.match_info)
        return func(self, *args, **kwargs)
    return new_func


class BaseApi(web.View):

    def __new__(cls, request):
        if request.method in hdrs.METH_ALL:
            method = request.method.lower()
            if hasattr(cls, method):
                # embed params from match_info
                embed_method = _embed_params(getattr(cls, method))
                setattr(cls, method, embed_method)
        return super(BaseApi, cls).__new__(cls)

    def output(self, data):
        return web.Response(text=data)

issue_title: Missing SVG config for mathjax
issue_body:
It appears that the bundled mathjax installation is missing the config files for SVG rendering. When right-clicking on a mathjax formula , and choosing Math Settings > Math Renderer > SVG, I get a an error

    404 GET /static/components/MathJax/jax/output/SVG/config.js?rev=2.5.3

Indeed it is easy to verify that the underlying directory in `site-packages` only contains `HTML-CSS/config.js`, but not `SVG/config.js`. The problem is that the choice for the SVG renderer is cached by the browser. As a result, viewing any subsequent notebooks results in the `404` error (which is generally not visible to the user) and completely deactivates the mathjax capabilities (a markdown cell with LaTeX  will not be rendered at all). This is then permanent; the only way to recover from the bug is to clear the browser cache.

I would strongly advocate to include the SVG renderer in the bundled mathjax installation, not only in order to avoid the above bug, but also because I still find that SVG-rendered mathjax looks nicer for operators with hats (as are commonly used in quantum mechanics).

def image_preprocessing(image):

issue_title: cached libraries don't update on new versions

**I fixed my issue by copying the updated wrapper into the cached folder.** 

I am assuming at some point in the installation of the sdk, or in running the python code the cached folder was created, so it might be worth looking into a way to check that this cached folder is either not created or is updated when needed.

issue_title: Add the ability to git clone a module.
issue_body:
I love that I can build a yaml file like this

    imports:
        test_module: test_module/

     git module test_module:
        url: git@github.com:user/test_module.git

 and run `peru sync` to grab this (and the many more submodules) I'd like to use for this project. More and more I find that I want to break projects into separate repositories with their own revision history.

I would love to have the option for peru to git clone the test_module into the target directory. This means I can add the target directory to my `.gitignore`, work on the super project while using the sub project (test_module in this case) and I have the option to work on the sub project if the super project needs a feature added / changed.

Basically this boils down to I want to decouple my sub projects from my super projects. Just because my super project needs to depend on all of it's sub projects does not mean those sub projects need to be pulled into the super projects source tree (git subtree) or tied to a single commit of each sub project (git submodules)

In my mind I love what sync does but I'd like to change how it initializes things.

issue_title: Registrar start up failure in Windows
issue_body:
In the Windows release, the registrar worker fails to start up because hardlinking the registrar lockfile results in an IOError in that particular boot2docker directory.

Fix is to move the lockfile to `/tmp/ `, but as of `0.17.0.7`, registration is *broken* on Windows. Windows users should upgrade once we release a hotfix (they can run `launcher pull` from the Blockstack docker shell once the hotfix is out)

We planned to use the new pip feature of selectors to install partial dependencies. That way, users who only want to use the simple sqlite backends, not a full MongoDB deployment, won't need to install pymongo.

```
pip install databroker  # minimal deps

This should get done before we tag.

issue_title: model-missing-unicode false positive for subclassed model
issue_body:
Hi

For this code block ([original source](https://github.com/mozilla/treeherder/blob/572c75d8ecdbbd385f1177284547d8410401140c/treeherder/model/models.py#L40-L42)):

```python
from django.db import models
from django.utils.encoding import python_2_unicode_compatible


@python_2_unicode_compatible
class NamedModel(models.Model):
    id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, unique=True)

    class Meta:
        abstract = True

    def __str__(self):
        return self.name


class Product(NamedModel):
    class Meta:
        db_table = 'product'
```

pylint with pylint_django reports:
```
treeherder/model/models.py:40,0: No __unicode__ method on model (Product) (W5101: model-missing-unicode)
```

The parent model already uses the `@python_2_unicode_compatible` decorator, so it seems that instead of W5101, this should be W5103?

The above was using:
Python 2.7.14
Django==1.11.12
pylint==1.8.4
pylint-django==0.9.4
pylint-plugin-utils==0.2.6
astroid==1.6.3

issue_title: Research Domain.dns
issue_body:
The semantics of the `Domain.dns` field are not well understood. As a result, the field is omitted. The API documentation mentions it, though. From the API documentation:

> POST /api/domains
> domain[dns_id]
> DNS proxy to use within this domain
> Value: number.

Research the semantics of this field and add it to `nailgun.entities` if appropriate.

## Get Started

Text here references BCL - a newcomer to .NET probably doesn't understand the acronym (I didn't until I looked it up online)

This section describes multiple licenses, so the plural form should probably be used here.

## Understand the relationship between .NET Core and the .NET Framework.

## Learning about ASP.NET and .NET Core

issue_body:
I have a class library project in .NET Core that targets two environments 
```
>  <PropertyGroup>
>        <TargetFrameworks>netcoreapp2.0;net4.6.1</TargetFrameworks>
>  </PropertyGroup>
```
All is working fine until after I install FxCopAnalyzers and suppress a warning (CA1707). 
```
>    <ItemGroup>
>         <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.FxCopAnalyzers" Version="2.6.0" />
>         <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer" Version="2.1.0" />
>         <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration" Version="2.1.0" />
>         <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.FileExtensions" Version="2.1.0" />
>         <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.Json" Version="2.1.0" />
>     </ItemGroup> 
```
When I add the warning suppression CA1707 the following section is automatically added to the project file
```
>  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(Configuration)|$(TargetFramework)|$(Platform)'   
>        =='Debug|netcoreapp2.0|AnyCPU'">
>     <NoWarn>1701;1702;1705; CA1707;</NoWarn>
>   </PropertyGroup>
```
After that the Manage Nuget Packages... on Dependencies is not working anymore. No installed nuget packages are shown at all and no updates are possible. issue_title: ./letsencrypt-auto --help is not very user-friendly
issue_body:
This overlaps with item #1286 but comes at things from a different angle.

According to https://letsencrypt.github.io/acme-spec/, the raison d'être for ACME is to provide a good user interface, to provide good usability.

From a UI point of view, it is not reasonable to require root privileges for ./letsencrypt-auto --help

It is irrelevant and pointless to say that because of the way it's written, it requires root privileges.  The relevant point is, it shouldn't be written that way.  A good UI should be written in such a way that it can provide an informative message no matter what.  One way (not the only way) of doing this would be to incorporate a helpful message within the letsencrypt-auto shell script ... and to check for the --help option before checking for root privileges.

issue_title: Problem with sending emails with emojis
issue_body:
I got an issue with emails that contain emojis. When I receive email with emojis
all emojies are displayed like a bunch of '??'. I tried to send the same data via Postman
and email I received wasn't broken. So it seems someting wrong happens on the way 
between this python client and API server.
By digging into source code of `Transmissions` part and by sniffing traffic
I found that python's json module encodes (dumps) 'not simple' non-ascii characters 
(such emojies) to '\uXXXX' notation as a surrogate pair (as expected):

```
>>> emoji = u"😄"
>>> emoji
u'\U0001f604'
>>> print json.dumps(emoji)
"\ud83d\ude04"
```

By sending this data I got this emoji looks like:

```
\xed\xa0\xbd\xed\xb8\x84
```

And it's of course wrong. I sniffed traffic between Postman and API server and I found
that Postman sends json in utf-8 (without escaping to \uXXXX):

```
\xf0\x9f\x98\x84
```

So I repeat this in Python and it worked:

```
>>> json.dumps(emoji, ensure_ascii=False).encode('utf-8')
'"\xf0\x9f\x98\x84"'
```

I don't know on what side problem is, but this is my workaround to handle this problem.
And this Issue is just a point on a problem and its possible solution.

PS. There is no problem with sending emails consist of simple chars like '\u2605'.

issue_title: tzwin does not accept some values returned by tzname
issue_body:
Some values returned by `tzname` are not valid for `tzwin`.  It appears `tzwin` doesn't like daylight savings time names even though `tzname` returns them.
```python
import datetime
import dateutil.tz

now = datetime.datetime.now()

tzwl_name = dateutil.tz.tzwinlocal().tzname(now)
print tzwl_name  # 'Eastern Daylight Time'

tzw_name = dateutil.tz.tzwin('Eastern Standard Time').tzname(now)
print tzw_name  # 'Eastern Daylight Time'

gettz_name = dateutil.tz.gettz(tzwl_name).tzname(now)
print gettz_name  # 'Eastern Standard Time'

dateutil.tz.tzwin(tzwl_name)  # raises WindowsError
```
```
Eastern Daylight Time
Eastern Daylight Time
Eastern Standard Time

    dateutil.tz.tzwin(name)
  File "C:\python\date.cur\dateutil\tz\win.py", line 92, in __init__
    "%s\%s" % (TZKEYNAME, name)) as tzkey:
WindowsError: [Error 2] The system cannot find the file specified

`tzname`s are inconsistent, however that appears to be a separate issue (see #143)

issue_title: SalesforceAutoField does not inherit from fields.AutoField
issue_body:
I'm running into an error running tests on a postgres (non-Salesforce) database. The issue is that django doesn't respect `SalesforceAutoField.get_internal_type`, and generates errors like the following:
```
IntegrityError: null value in column "Id" violates not-null constraint
```

Django is not properly viewing the field as an auto-increment primary key. I think this is caused by a bug in Django [here](https://github.com/django/django/blob/01ab84c61330ffa5ac87c637249611c5e5343e57/django/db/models/base.py#L800), where it checks whether the field is an instance of `AutoField` instead of checking that `f.get_internal_type() == 'AutoField'`. The Django source code seems to be inconsistent on this point, sometimes checking `get_internal_type`, and sometimes checking whether the field is an instance of `AutoField`. I'm going to submit a patch with test cases to Django, but an easy fix in `django-salesforce` itself would be to have `SalesforceAutofield` inherit from `fields.AutoField`.

Set your settings file to use a postgres db:
```python
DATABASES = {
    'default': {
        'NAME': 'something',
        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql_psycopg2',
        'USER': 'django',
        'PASSWORD': 'secret',
    },
}

```python
from salesforce import models as sf_models
from django.db import models

class Foo(sf_models.Model):
    class Meta:
        managed=True
```

 ```
$ DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=django_salesforce_example python manage.py syncdb
$ DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=django_salesforce_example python manage.py shell

```python

```

issue_title: Start should not be 0 after normalizing variants inserted before the start of transcript
issue_body:
When insertions occur before the first nucleotide of the transcript, the current normalizer would result in n.0_1insXXX, then the interval validator would fail as the start should never be 0. For example, 

when normalizing variant ''NM_212556.2:c.1delinsCA", the normalizer would firstly covert to n. representation of this variant "NM_212556.2:n.1delinsCA" (The CDS of this transcript starts just from 1 and the first nucleotide is A), then normalized to "NM_212556.2:n.0_1insC". When the normalizer tries to convert back to c. variants, the internal validator in n_to_c would raise exception as the start should not be 0.

issue_title: Two Way Serialization
issue_body:
Trying to cope with this without writing two schemes for the same stuff.

Let's say I'm taking in one API and it gives me something like this:

```
{ 
 "CamelCased": "Stuff",
 "somethingElse": "More stuff",
 "OhLookAList": [1,2,3,4]
}
```

And I'm repackaging it, doing some fun stuff internally, and then dumping it out the other side into my own API. Don't ask why I can't keep the same names. 

```
{
 "camel_cased": "That's a lie now.",
 "something_else": "More stuff",
 "oh_look_a_list": [1,2,3,4]
}
```

Normally I'd just use dump_to/load_from and be done with it all...however, I need to reverse the flow and send information from my API back to the external API. So someone will post:

```
{
 "camel_cased": "That's a lie now.",
 "something_else": "More stuff",
 "oh_look_a_list": [1,2,3,4]
}
```

And the external API will get...

```
{ 
 "CamelCased": "Stuff",
 "somethingElse": "More stuff",
 "OhLookAList": [1,2,3,4]
}
```

In the example text for marshmallow-enum I used a (nasty, in my opinion) pre_load/pre_dump workaround that looked at the schema context to see if the incoming data was the internal source or external source, and then switched a value on the field. However, I'm hesitant to do that for an _entire_ schema.

The best way I can think to handle this with Marshmallow is to just some sort of schema factory that'd create external/internal schemas on demand, potentially a less crappy version of this:

``` python
def make_two_way_schema(context):
     class SomeSchema(Schema):
         id = fields.Str(load_from=context['id']['read'], dump_to=context['id']['write'])
     return SomeSchema(...)
```

However, I'm unplussed about this idea or the prospect of managing two sets of schemas. I could see manufacturing the schemas before hand -- using the above mechanism -- and then at dump/load time choose the correct one. Maybe something like...

``` python
def make_two_way_schema(mapping):
      internal_to_external = ...
      external_to_internal = ...

      def schema_choose(...): # all the same args as Schema.__init__
          return internal_to_external if context['source'] == 'internal' else external_to_internal
      return schema_choose
```

This strikes me as the lesser of two evils but not by much... Any ideas?

issue_title: Add option to throw validation error on extra keys

I've been using the following implementation (taken from https://github.com/sloria/webargs/issues/87#issuecomment-183949558):

```python
class BaseSchema(Schema):

    @pre_load

        if not isinstance(in_data, dict):

        extra_args = [key for key in in_data.keys() if key not in self.fields]

issue_title: Empty Only Treated as None
issue_body:
When the `only` parameter is an empty list/set, it causes all the fields to be de/serialized like `None`. The implementation of 2.x and 3.0 are not in compliance with their respective docs.

> **only** (_tuple_) – A list or tuple of fields to serialize. If **None**, all fields will be serialized.

```py
class TestSchema(Schema):
    foo = fields.Field()
sch = TestSchema(only=())
data = dict(foo='bar')
result = sch.dump(data)
assert 'foo' not in result

# assert 'foo' not in {'foo': 'bar'}
```

This could create a security vulnerability if an application was dynamically generating the field set based on security role. A filter that was meant to hide everything would inadvertently show everything.
